summary:
All patients with acute coronary syndromes requiring percutaneous intervention should undergo dual antiplatelet therapy—typically including aspirin plus clopidogrel—for minimum 12 months to reduce risk of stent thrombosis; spironolactone remains contraindicated in those with preserved left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF >50%).  

The correct answer is C.
